what_it_does: Defines a single module-level ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict declaring a 'cloning' attachment type that binds a point-in-time cloning capability to a channel; target_kind is 'scope' (the source session/scope clones are minted from), multi=True.
observations: One module = one ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict with keys id, label, target_kind, multi, desc.
target_kind is 'scope' meaning the target is a source session/scope rather than a concrete address.
multi=True allows many clone attachments per channel.
Mentions 'era-clones minted from the target source' — ties to a cloning/time-travel concept.
Module docstring restates the id's purpose.
standouts: Models cloning as an attachment bound to a channel, with the clone source expressed as a scope target.
conventions: Follows a uniform single-dict descriptor pattern (id/label/target_kind/multi/desc) shared across attachment_types modules.
Inline comment annotates what target_kind means for this type.
notes: A tiny declarative registry entry: the 'cloning' channel-attachment type, target = a scope (clone source), many-per-channel.
questions: What consumer reads ATTACHMENT_TYPE and how is target_kind='scope' resolved into an actual clone source?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring says it declares the cloning attachment type and the dict does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Channels as the binding point for capabilities
intention_signals: Aims to make cloning a first-class, channel-attachable capability described as data so a generic attachment system can list/resolve it.
novelty: The scope-targeted 'era-clones' framing of cloning as a channel attachment.
contribution: Contributes the declarative definition of the cloning attachment type, including its scope target semantics and multi-cardinality.
summary_for_embedding: A single-dict descriptor module declaring the 'cloning' channel attachment type whose target is a scope (clone source session) with multi=True.
intention_for_embedding: To register point-in-time/era cloning as a channel-bound attachment type expressed as data for a generic attachment system.
